revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{日本の古墳 |名称=小二子古墳 |所在地=[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]西大室町2545 |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=全長38m、後円部径30m...'
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=小二子古墳 |所在地=[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]西大室町2545 |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=全長38m、後円部径30m、高さ5m、前方部幅18m |築造年代=6世紀後半 |埋葬施設=横穴式石室 |出土品=円筒埴輪、形象埴輪、須恵器、武器、装身具 |史跡指定=国の史跡 }}
'''小二子古墳'''（しょうふたごこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]西大室町にある[[古墳]]。[[大室古墳群]]の一つ。
== 概要 == 墳丘長38メートルの前方後円墳で、主軸を北東131°に向けている。墳丘は2段構築となっている。墳丘の一部は地山を削り出して造成され、葺石は施されていない。墳丘の周囲は盾型の[[堀]]が巡らされ、堀を入れた長さは44メートルに達する。前二子古墳のすぐ東側に[[後二子古墳]]が隣接している。 大室古墳群整備の際、小二子古墳は墳丘の残りがよくなかったため、築造時の状況を復元し展示する資料を得る目的で全面的な発掘調査が行われた。現在は調査結果を基に築造時のように墳丘に埴輪が樹立された状態に復元されている。
== 出土品 == 石室は明治時代に盗掘を受け、天井部分が破壊されていたが、ガラス製小玉14、金銅製耳環3、直刀3、刀子1、鉄鏃10、弓金具9、須恵器1が出土した。石室前面からは6世紀後半の特徴を示す土師器坏8と須恵器提瓶1が出土している。前方部墳頂から人物埴輪、後円部墳頂から器財埴輪、テラス面から円筒埴輪列が検出された。
== 参考文献 == * 前原豊 『東国大豪族の威勢・大室古墳群〔群馬〕』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」063 新泉社 2009年
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[関東の史跡一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しょうふたごこふん}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:群馬県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:前橋市の歴史]] {{japanese-history-stub|}}

{{日本の古墳 |名称=小二子古墳 |所在地=[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]西大室町2545 |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=全長38m、後円部径30m、高さ5m、前方部幅18m |築造年代=6世紀後半 |埋葬施設=横穴式石室 |出土品=円筒埴輪、形象埴輪、須恵器、土師器、武器、装身具 |史跡指定=国の史跡 }}

== 参考文献 == * 前原豊 『東国大豪族の威勢・大室古墳群〔群馬〕』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」063、新泉社、2009年
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[関東の史跡一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しようふたここふん}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:群馬県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:前橋市の歴史]] {{japanese-history-stub|}}

{{日本の古墳 |画像=[[File:Shofutago-kofun 2.jpg|300px]] |名称=小二子古墳 |所在地=[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]西大室町2545 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|23|14.9|N|139|12|1.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=全長38m<ref>前橋市教育委員会編「前橋市 大室古墳群 -前二子古墳・中二子古墳・後二子古墳・小二子古墳-」、2013年</ref>、後円部径30m、高さ5m、前方部幅18m |築造年代=6世紀後半 |埋葬施設=横穴式石室 |出土品=円筒埴輪、形象埴輪、須恵器、土師器、武器、装身具 |史跡指定=国の史跡 }} [[File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|thumb|小二子古墳]] '''小二子古墳'''（しょうふたごこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]西大室町にある[[古墳]]。[[大室古墳群 (前橋市)|大室古墳群]]の一つ。[[1927年]][[4月8日]]、隣接する[[後二子古墳]]と共に国の史跡に指定（指定名称は「'''後二子古墳ならびに小古墳'''」）<ref>[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index_pc.asp 国指定文化財等データベース 文化庁]</ref>。
== 概要 == 墳丘長38メートルの前方後円墳で、主軸を北東131°に向けている。墳丘は2段構築となっている。墳丘の一部は地山を削り出して造成され、[[葺石]]は施されていない。墳丘の周囲は盾型の[[堀]]が巡らされ、堀を入れた長さは44メートルに達する。テラス面には80～90本の[[円筒埴輪]]列、前方部頂には人物を中心とした形象埴輪列、後円部頂には器財を中心にした形象埴輪が配列されていた<ref name=maehara>前原豊 「埴輪が林立する小二子古墳」『東国大豪族の威勢・大室古墳群〔群馬〕』 （シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」063）、新泉社、2009年</ref>。
後二子古墳とほぼ同様の東西軸を持ち、つくられた時期も同じ頃で、後二子古墳と関わりの深い人物の墓と考えられる<ref name=maehara />。
大室古墳群整備の際、小二子古墳は墳丘の残りがよくなかったため、築造時の状況を復元し展示する資料を得る目的で全面的な[[発掘調査]]が行われた。現在は調査結果を基に築造時のように墳丘に埴輪が樹立された状態に復元されている<ref name=maehara />。
== 埋葬施設 == 半地下式の無袖型横穴式石室である。石室は[[明治時代]]に[[盗掘]]を受け、天井部分が破壊されていたが、全長6メートル、奥壁幅1.8メートル、高さ1.8メートルと復元されている。ガラス製小玉14、金銅製耳環3、[[直刀]]3、[[刀子]]1、[[鉄鏃]]10、[[弓]]金具9、[[須恵器]]1が出土した。石室前面からは[[6世紀]]後半の特徴を示す[[土師器]]坏8と須恵器提瓶1が出土している。石室両側からは焼土が検出され、墓前祭祀が行われたことをうかがわせる<ref name=maehara />。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[関東の史跡一覧]]

revision.comment: 巻き戻し：不適当な内部リンク修正につき。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |画像=[[File:Shofutago-kofun 2.jpg|300px]] |名称=小二子古墳 |所属=[[大室古墳群 (前橋市)|大室古墳群]] |所在地=[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]西大室町2545 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=全長38m<ref>前橋市教育委員会編「前橋市 大室古墳群 -前二子古墳・中二子古墳・後二子古墳・小二子古墳-」、2013年</ref>、後円部径30m、高さ5m、前方部幅18m |築造年代=6世紀後半 |埋葬施設=横穴式石室 |出土品=円筒埴輪、形象埴輪、須恵器、土師器、武器、装身具 |史跡指定=国の史跡 }} [[File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|thumb|小二子古墳]] '''小二子古墳'''（しょうふたごこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]西大室町にある[[古墳]]。[[大室古墳群 (前橋市)|大室古墳群]]の一つ。[[1927年]]（昭和2年）[[4月8日]]、隣接する[[後二子古墳]]と共に国の史跡に指定（指定名称は「'''後二子古墳ならびに小古墳'''」）<ref>[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index_pc.asp 国指定文化財等データベース 文化庁]</ref>。
== 概要 == 墳丘長38メートルの前方後円墳で、主軸を北東131°に向けている。墳丘は2段構築となっている。墳丘の一部は地山を削り出して造成され、[[葺石]]は施されていない。墳丘の周囲は盾型の[[堀]]が巡らされ、堀を入れた長さは44メートルに達する。テラス面には80～90本の[[円筒埴輪]]列、前方部頂には人物を中心とした形象埴輪列、後円部頂には器財を中心にした形象埴輪が配列されていた<ref name=maehara>前原豊 「埴輪が林立する小二子古墳」『東国大豪族の威勢・大室古墳群〔群馬〕』 （シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」063）、新泉社、2009年</ref>。
後二子古墳とほぼ同様の東西軸を持ち、つくられた時期も同じ頃で、後二子古墳と関わりの深い人物の墓と考えられる<ref name=maehara />。
大室古墳群整備の際、小二子古墳は墳丘の残りがよくなかったため、築造時の状況を復元し展示する資料を得る目的で全面的な[[発掘調査]]が行われた。現在は調査結果を基に築造時のように墳丘に埴輪が樹立された状態に復元されている<ref name=maehara />。
== 埋葬施設 == 半地下式の無袖型横穴式石室である。石室は[[明治時代]]に[[盗掘]]を受け、天井部分が破壊されていたが、全長6メートル、奥壁幅1.8メートル、高さ1.8メートルと復元されている。ガラス製小玉14、金銅製耳環3、[[直刀]]3、[[刀子]]1、[[鉄鏃]]10、[[弓]]金具9、[[須恵器]]1が出土した。石室前面からは[[6世紀]]後半の特徴を示す[[土師器]]坏8と須恵器提瓶1が出土している。石室両側からは焼土が検出され、墓前祭祀が行われたことをうかがわせる<ref name=maehara />。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[関東の史跡一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しようふたここふん}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:群馬県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:前橋市の歴史]] {{japanese-history-stub|}}

後二子古墳とほぼ同様の東西軸を持ち、つくられた時期も同じ頃で、後二子古墳と関わりの深い人物の墓と考えられる<ref name=maehara />。
大室古墳群整備の際、小二子古墳は墳丘の残りがよくなかったため、築造時の状況を復元し展示する資料を得る目的で全面的な[[発掘調査]]が行われた。現在は調査結果を基に築造時のように墳丘に埴輪が樹立された状態に復元されている<ref name=maehara />。
== 埋葬施設 == 半地下式の無袖型横穴式石室である。石室は[[明治時代]]に[[盗掘]]を受け、天井部分が破壊されていたが、全長6メートル、奥壁幅1.8メートル、高さ1.8メートルと復元されている。ガラス製小玉14、金銅製耳環3、[[直刀]]3、[[刀子]]1、[[鉄鏃]]10、[[弓]]金具9、[[須恵器]]1が出土した。石室前面からは[[6世紀]]後半の特徴を示す[[土師器]]坏8と須恵器提瓶1が出土している。石室両側からは焼土が検出され、墓前祭祀が行われたことをうかがわせる<ref name=maehara />。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[関東地方の史跡一覧]]

{{日本の古墳 |画像=[[File:Shofutago-kofun 2.jpg|300px]] |名称=小二子古墳 |所属=[[大室古墳群 (前橋市)|大室古墳群]] |所在地=[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]西大室町2545 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長38m<ref>前橋市教育委員会編「前橋市 大室古墳群 -前二子古墳・中二子古墳・後二子古墳・小二子古墳-」、2013年</ref>、後円部径30m、高さ5m、前方部幅18m |築造時期=[[6世紀]]後半 |埋葬施設=[[横穴式石室]] |出土品=円筒埴輪、形象埴輪、須恵器、土師器、武器、装身具 |史跡=国の[[史跡]]「後二子古墳ならびに小古墳」に包含 }} [[File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|thumb|小二子古墳]] '''小二子古墳'''（しょうふたごこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]西大室町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[大室古墳群 (前橋市)|大室古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。[[後二子古墳]]と合わせて国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（指定名称は「後二子古墳ならびに小古墳」）<ref>[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index_pc.asp 国指定文化財等データベース 文化庁]</ref>。
== 概要 == 墳丘長38メートルの前方後円墳で、主軸を北東131°に向けている。墳丘は2段構築となっている。墳丘の一部は地山を削り出して造成され、[[葺石]]は施されていない。墳丘の周囲は盾型の[[堀]]が巡らされ、堀を入れた長さは44メートルに達する。テラス面には80～90本の[[円筒埴輪]]列、前方部頂には人物を中心とした形象埴輪列、後円部頂には器財を中心にした形象埴輪が配列されていた<ref name=maehara>前原豊 「埴輪が林立する小二子古墳」『東国大豪族の威勢・大室古墳群〔群馬〕』 （シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」063）、新泉社、2009年</ref>。
後二子古墳とほぼ同様の東西軸を持ち、つくられた時期も同じ頃で、後二子古墳と関わりの深い人物の墓と考えられる<ref name=maehara />。
大室古墳群整備の際、小二子古墳は墳丘の残りがよくなかったため、築造時の状況を復元し展示する資料を得る目的で全面的な[[発掘調査]]が行われた。現在は調査結果を基に築造時のように墳丘に埴輪が樹立された状態に復元されている<ref name=maehara />。
== 埋葬施設 == 半地下式の無袖型横穴式石室である。石室は[[明治時代]]に[[盗掘]]を受け、天井部分が破壊されていたが、全長6メートル、奥壁幅1.8メートル、高さ1.8メートルと復元されている。ガラス製小玉14、金銅製耳環3、[[直刀]]3、[[刀子]]1、[[鉄鏃]]10、[[弓]]金具9、[[須恵器]]1が出土した。石室前面からは[[6世紀]]後半の特徴を示す[[土師器]]坏8と須恵器提瓶1が出土している。石室両側からは焼土が検出され、墓前祭祀が行われたことをうかがわせる<ref name=maehara />。
== 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 後二子古墳ならびに小古墳 - 1927年（昭和2年）4月8日指定<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|529|後二子古墳ならびに小古墳}}</ref>。

{{日本の古墳 |画像=[[File:Shofutago-kofun 2.jpg|300px]] |名称=小二子古墳 |所属=[[大室古墳群 (前橋市)|大室古墳群]] |所在地=[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]西大室町2545 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長38m前橋市教育委員会編「前橋市　大室古墳群 -前二子古墳・中二子古墳・後二子古墳・小二子古墳-」、2013年、後円部径30m、高さ5m、前方部幅18m |築造時期=[[6世紀]]後半 |埋葬施設=[[横穴式石室]] |出土品=円筒埴輪、形象埴輪、須恵器、土師器、武器、装身具 |史跡=国の[[史跡]]「後二子古墳ならびに小古墳」に包含 }} [[File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|thumb|小二子古墳]] '''小二子古墳'''（しょうふたごこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]西大室町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[大室古墳群 (前橋市)|大室古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。[[後二子古墳]]と合わせて国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（指定名称は「後二子古墳ならびに小古墳」）[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index_pc.asp 国指定文化財等データベース　文化庁]。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+[[大室古墳群 (前橋市)|大室古墳群]]の変遷 !古墳名 !形状 !規模 !埋葬施設 !築造時期 |- |[[前二子古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長94m||両袖式[[横穴式石室]]||[[6世紀]]初頭 |- |[[中二子古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長111m||[[横穴式石室]]?||6世紀前半 |- |[[後二子古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長85m||両袖式[[横穴式石室]]||6世紀後半 |- |'''小二子古墳'''||前方後円墳||墳丘長38m||無袖式[[横穴式石室]]||6世紀後半 |}
墳丘長38メートルの前方後円墳で、主軸を北東131°に向けている。墳丘は2段構築となっている。墳丘の一部は地山を削り出して造成され、[[葺石]]は施されていない。墳丘の周囲は盾型の[[堀]]が巡らされ、堀を入れた長さは44メートルに達する。テラス面には80～90本の[[円筒埴輪]]列、前方部頂には人物を中心とした形象埴輪列、後円部頂には器財を中心にした形象埴輪が配列されていた前原豊 「埴輪が林立する小二子古墳」『東国大豪族の威勢・大室古墳群〔群馬〕』 （シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」063）、新泉社、2009年。 後二子古墳とほぼ同様の東西軸を持ち、つくられた時期も同じ頃で、後二子古墳と関わりの深い人物の墓と考えられる。 大室古墳群整備の際、小二子古墳は墳丘の残りがよくなかったため、築造時の状況を復元し展示する資料を得る目的で全面的な[[発掘調査]]が行われた。現在は調査結果を基に築造時のように墳丘に埴輪が樹立された状態に復元されている。 == 埋葬施設 == 半地下式の無袖型横穴式石室である。石室は[[明治時代]]に[[盗掘]]を受け、天井部分が破壊されていたが、全長6メートル、奥壁幅1.8メートル、高さ1.8メートルと復元されている。ガラス製小玉14、金銅製耳環3、[[直刀]]3、[[刀子]]1、[[鉄鏃]]10、[[弓]]金具9、[[須恵器]]1が出土した。石室前面からは[[6世紀]]後半の特徴を示す[[土師器]]坏8と須恵器提瓶1が出土している。石室両側からは焼土が検出され、墓前祭祀が行われたことをうかがわせる。 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 後二子古墳ならびに小古墳 - 1927年（昭和2年）4月8日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|529|後二子古墳ならびに小古墳}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 関連項目 =- * [[大室古墳群 (前橋市)]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shofutago Kofun}} * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html 大室古墳群] - 前橋市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:しようふたここふん}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:前橋市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:群馬県にある国指定の史跡]] {{japanese-history-stub|}}

{{日本の古墳 |画像=[[File:Shofutago-kofun 2.jpg|300px]] |名称=小二子古墳 |所属=[[大室古墳群 (前橋市)|大室古墳群]] |所在地=[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]西大室町2545 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長38m前橋市教育委員会編「前橋市　大室古墳群 -前二子古墳・中二子古墳・後二子古墳・小二子古墳-」、2013年、後円部径30m、高さ5m、前方部幅18m |築造時期=[[6世紀]]後半 |埋葬施設=[[横穴式石室]] |出土品=円筒埴輪、形象埴輪、須恵器、土師器、武器、装身具 |史跡=国の[[史跡]]「後二子古墳ならびに小古墳」に包含 }} [[File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|thumb|小二子古墳]] '''小二子古墳'''（しょうふたごこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]西大室町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[大室古墳群 (前橋市)|大室古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。[[後二子古墳]]と合わせて国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（指定名称は「後二子古墳ならびに小古墳」）[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index_pc.asp 国指定文化財等データベース　文化庁]。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+[[大室古墳群 (前橋市)|大室古墳群]]の変遷 !古墳名 !形状 !規模 !埋葬施設 !築造時期 |- |[[前二子古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長94m||両袖式[[横穴式石室]]||[[6世紀]]初頭 |- |[[中二子古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長111m||[[横穴式石室]]?||6世紀前半 |- |[[後二子古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長85m||両袖式[[横穴式石室]]||6世紀後半 |- |'''小二子古墳'''||前方後円墳||墳丘長38m||無袖式[[横穴式石室]]||6世紀後半 |}
墳丘長38メートルの前方後円墳で、主軸を北東131°に向けている。墳丘は2段構築となっている。墳丘の一部は地山を削り出して造成され、[[葺石]]は施されていない。墳丘の周囲は盾型の[[堀]]が巡らされ、堀を入れた長さは44メートルに達する。テラス面には80～90本の[[円筒埴輪]]列、前方部頂には人物を中心とした形象埴輪列、後円部頂には器財を中心にした形象埴輪が配列されていた前原豊 「埴輪が林立する小二子古墳」『東国大豪族の威勢・大室古墳群〔群馬〕』 （シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」063）、新泉社、2009年。 後二子古墳とほぼ同様の東西軸を持ち、つくられた時期も同じ頃で、後二子古墳と関わりの深い人物の墓と考えられる。 大室古墳群整備の際、小二子古墳は墳丘の残りがよくなかったため、築造時の状況を復元し展示する資料を得る目的で全面的な[[発掘調査]]が行われた。現在は調査結果を基に築造時のように墳丘に埴輪が樹立された状態に復元されている。 == 埋葬施設 == 半地下式の無袖型横穴式石室である。石室は[[明治時代]]に[[盗掘]]を受け、天井部分が破壊されていたが、全長6メートル、奥壁幅1.8メートル、高さ1.8メートルと復元されている。ガラス製小玉14、金銅製耳環3、[[直刀]]3、[[刀子]]1、[[鉄鏃]]10、[[弓]]金具9、[[須恵器]]1が出土した。石室前面からは[[6世紀]]後半の特徴を示す[[土師器]]坏8と須恵器提瓶1が出土している。石室両側からは焼土が検出され、墓前祭祀が行われたことをうかがわせる。 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 後二子古墳ならびに小古墳 - 1927年（昭和2年）4月8日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|529|後二子古墳ならびに小古墳}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[大室古墳群 (前橋市)]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shofutago Kofun}} * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html 大室古墳群] - 前橋市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:しようふたここふん}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:前橋市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:群馬県にある国指定の史跡]] {{japanese-history-stub|}}

{{日本の古墳 |画像=[[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘
（右に後円部・石室開口部、左に前方部） |名称=小二子古墳 |所属=[[大室古墳群 (前橋市)|大室古墳群]] |所在地=[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]西大室町2545 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長38m前橋市教育委員会編「前橋市　大室古墳群 -前二子古墳・中二子古墳・後二子古墳・小二子古墳-」、2013年、後円部径30m、高さ5m、前方部幅18m |築造時期=[[6世紀]]後半 |埋葬施設=[[横穴式石室]] |出土品=円筒埴輪、形象埴輪、須恵器、土師器、武器、装身具 |史跡=国の[[史跡]]「後二子古墳ならびに小古墳」に包含 }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|{{center|墳丘}}]] '''小二子古墳'''（しょうふたごこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]西大室町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[大室古墳群 (前橋市)|大室古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。[[後二子古墳]]と合わせて国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（指定名称は「後二子古墳ならびに小古墳」）[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index 国指定文化財等データベース　文化庁]。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+[[大室古墳群 (前橋市)|大室古墳群]]の前方後円墳4基 !古墳名 !形状 !規模 !埋葬施設 !築造時期 |- |[[前二子古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長94m||両袖式[[横穴式石室]]||[[6世紀]]初頭 |- |[[中二子古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長111m||横穴式石室?||6世紀前半 |- |[[後二子古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長85m||両袖式横穴式石室||6世紀後半 |- |'''小二子古墳'''||前方後円墳||墳丘長38m||無袖式横穴式石室||6世紀後半 |}
墳丘長38メートルの前方後円墳で、主軸を北東131°に向けている。墳丘は2段構築となっている。墳丘の一部は地山を削り出して造成され、[[葺石]]は施されていない。墳丘の周囲は盾型の[[堀]]が巡らされ、堀を入れた長さは44メートルに達する。テラス面には80～90本の[[円筒埴輪]]列、前方部頂には人物を中心とした形象埴輪列、後円部頂には器財を中心にした形象埴輪が配列されていた前原豊 「埴輪が林立する小二子古墳」『東国大豪族の威勢・大室古墳群〔群馬〕』 （シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」063）、新泉社、2009年。 後二子古墳とほぼ同様の東西軸を持ち、つくられた時期も同じ頃で、後二子古墳と関わりの深い人物の墓と考えられる。 大室古墳群整備の際、小二子古墳は墳丘の残りがよくなかったため、築造時の状況を復元し展示する資料を得る目的で全面的な[[発掘調査]]が行われた。現在は調査結果を基に築造時のように墳丘に埴輪が樹立された状態に復元されている。 == 埋葬施設 == 半地下式の無袖型横穴式石室である。石室は[[明治時代]]に[[盗掘]]を受け、天井部分が破壊されていたが、全長6メートル、奥壁幅1.8メートル、高さ1.8メートルと復元されている。ガラス製小玉14、金銅製耳環3、[[直刀]]3、[[刀子]]1、[[鉄鏃]]10、[[弓]]金具9、[[須恵器]]1が出土した。石室前面からは[[6世紀]]後半の特徴を示す[[土師器]]坏8と須恵器提瓶1が出土している。石室両側からは焼土が検出され、墓前祭祀が行われたことをうかがわせる。 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 後二子古墳ならびに小古墳 - 1927年（昭和2年）4月8日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|529|後二子古墳ならびに小古墳}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[大室古墳群 (前橋市)]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shofutago Kofun}} * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html 大室古墳群] - 前橋市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:しようふたここふん}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:前橋市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:群馬県にある国指定の史跡]] {{japanese-history-stub|}}

{{日本の古墳 |画像=[[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘
（右に後円部・石室開口部、左に前方部） |名称=小二子古墳 |所属=[[大室古墳群 (前橋市)|大室古墳群]] |所在地=[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]西大室町2545 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長38m前橋市教育委員会編「前橋市　大室古墳群 -前二子古墳・中二子古墳・後二子古墳・小二子古墳-」、2013年、後円部径30m、高さ5m、前方部幅18m |築造時期=[[6世紀]]後半 |埋葬施設=[[横穴式石室]] |出土品=円筒埴輪、形象埴輪、須恵器、土師器、武器、装身具 |史跡=国の[[史跡]]「後二子古墳ならびに小古墳」に包含 }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|墳丘}}]] '''小二子古墳'''（しょうふたごこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]西大室町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[大室古墳群 (前橋市)|大室古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。[[後二子古墳]]と合わせて国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（指定名称は「後二子古墳ならびに小古墳」）[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index 国指定文化財等データベース　文化庁]。 == 概要 ==

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese burial mound | image=[[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus
(Rear circular mound with a burial chamber opening on the right; front rectangular mound on the left) | name=Shōfutago Kofun | cluster=[[Ōmuro Burial Mounds (Maebashi)|Ōmuro Burial Mounds]] | location=[[Nishi-Ōmuro-chō]], [[Maebashi City]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type=[[Keyhole-shaped burial mound]] | dimensions=Mound length: 38mCompiled by Maebashi City Board of Education, "Ōmuro Burial Mounds in Maebashi City - Shofutago, Nakafutago, Ushirofutago, and Ko-futago Burial Mounds," 2013; Rear circular mound diameter: 30m, height: 5m; Front rectangular mound width: 18m | period=[[Late 6th century]] | burial=[[Corridor-style stone chamber]] | artifacts=Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue ware, Haji ware, weapons, personal ornaments | designation=Included in the National [[Historic Sites of Japan|Historic Site]] "Ushirofutago and Small Burial Mounds" }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tumulus}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a [[burial mound]] located in [[Nishi-Ōmuro-chō]], [[Maebashi City]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a [[keyhole-shaped burial mound]] that forms part of the [[Ōmuro Burial Mounds (Maebashi)|Ōmuro Burial Mounds]] cluster. Together with the [[Ushirofutago Kofun]], it is designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] under the name "Ushirofutago and Small Burial Mounds."[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Agency for Cultural Affairs: National Cultural Properties Database] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Keyhole-shaped Burial Mounds in the [[Ōmuro Burial Mounds (Maebashi)|Ōmuro Burial Mounds]] !Name !Shape !Size !Burial Facility !Construction Period |- |[[Mae-Futago Kofun]]||[[Keyhole-shaped burial mound]]||Mound length: 94m||Corridor-style stone chamber with sidewalls||[[Early 6th century]] |- |[[Naka-Futago Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 111m||Possibly a corridor-style stone chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |[[Ushiro-Futago Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 85m||Corridor-style stone chamber with sidewalls||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 38m||Corridor-style stone chamber without sidewalls||Late 6th century |}
The mound is 38 meters long and aligned along a northeast axis of 131°. It is constructed in two tiers. Parts of the mound were carved out of the natural ground, and it was not covered with fukiishi (surface stones). A shield-shaped [[moat]] surrounds the mound, reaching 44 meters in total length when including the moat. Cylindrical [[haniwa]] were arranged on terraces (80–90 in total), while figurative haniwa depicting people were placed on the front section, and artifact-themed haniwa were positioned on the rear circular section.Maehara Toyo, "Cylindrical Haniwa at Shōfutago Kofun," in ''The Prestige of Eastern Japan’s Great Clans: The Ōmuro Burial Mounds [Gunma],'' Series "Learning from Archaeological Sites" 063, Shinsensha, 2009 The mound shares its orientation with the Ushirofutago Kofun and was constructed around the same time, suggesting a close association with the individual buried in the Ushirofutago Kofun. During restoration efforts at the Ōmuro Burial Mounds, the Shōfutago Kofun underwent extensive excavation due to its poor preservation. Findings from these excavations allowed a faithful restoration of the mound, complete with re-erected haniwa to replicate its original appearance. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber is a semi-underground corridor-style stone chamber without sidewalls. Although the chamber was damaged by [[looting]] during the [[Meiji era]], it has been restored to its original dimensions: total length of 6 meters, back wall width of 1.8 meters, and height of 1.8 meters. Artifacts recovered include 14 glass beads, three gilt-bronze earrings, three [[straight swords]], one [[dagger]], ten [[iron arrowheads]], nine bow fittings, and one piece of [[Sue ware]]. Additional finds in front of the chamber included eight Haji ware bowls and one Sue ware jar, characteristic of the late 6th century. Burnt soil detected near the chamber suggests that funerary rituals were performed at the site. == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Ushirofutago and Small Burial Mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|529|Ushirofutago and Small Burial Mounds}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[Ōmuro Burial Mounds (Maebashi)]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shofutago Kofun}} * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Burial Mounds] - Maebashi City website [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[ja:小二子古墳]]

{{Infobox Japanese burial mound | image=[[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus
(Rear circular mound with a burial chamber opening on the right; front rectangular mound on the left) | name=Shōfutago Kofun | cluster=[[Ōmuro Burial Mounds (Maebashi)|Ōmuro Burial Mounds]] | location=[[Nishi-Ōmuro-chō]], [[Maebashi City]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type=[[Keyhole-shaped burial mound]] | dimensions=Mound length: 38mCompiled by Maebashi City Board of Education, "Ōmuro Burial Mounds in Maebashi City - Shofutago, Nakafutago, Ushirofutago, and Ko-futago Burial Mounds," 2013; Rear circular mound diameter: 30m, height: 5m; Front rectangular mound width: 18m | period=[[Late 6th century]] | burial=[[Corridor-style stone chamber]] | artifacts=Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue ware, Haji ware, weapons, personal ornaments | designation=Included in the National [[Historic Sites of Japan|Historic Site]] "Ushirofutago and Small Burial Mounds" }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tumulus}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a [[burial mound]] located in [[Nishi-Ōmuro-chō]], [[Maebashi City]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a [[keyhole-shaped burial mound]] that forms part of the [[Ōmuro Burial Mounds (Maebashi)|Ōmuro Burial Mounds]] cluster. Together with the [[Ushirofutago Kofun]], it is designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] under the name "Ushirofutago and Small Burial Mounds."[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Agency for Cultural Affairs: National Cultural Properties Database] == Overview ==

{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Keyhole-shaped Burial Mounds in the [[Ōmuro Burial Mounds (Maebashi)|Ōmuro Burial Mounds]] !Name !type !Size !Burial Facility !Construction Period |- |[[Mae-Futago Kofun]]||[[Keyhole-shaped burial mound]]||Mound length: 94m||Corridor-style stone chamber with sidewalls||[[Early 6th century]] |- |[[Naka-Futago Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 111m||Possibly a corridor-style stone chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |[[Ushiro-Futago Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 85m||Corridor-style stone chamber with sidewalls||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Keyhole-shaped burial mound||Mound length: 38m||Corridor-style stone chamber without sidewalls||Late 6th century |}
The mound is 38 meters long and aligned along a northeast axis of 131°. It is constructed in two tiers. Parts of the mound were carved out of the natural ground, and it was not covered with fukiishi (surface stones). A shield-shaped [[moat]] surrounds the mound, reaching 44 meters in total length when including the moat. Cylindrical [[haniwa]] were arranged on terraces (80–90 in total), while figurative haniwa depicting people were placed on the front section, and artifact-themed haniwa were positioned on the rear circular section.Maehara Toyo, "Cylindrical Haniwa at Shōfutago Kofun," in ''The Prestige of Eastern Japan’s Great Clans: The Ōmuro Burial Mounds [Gunma],'' Series "Learning from Archaeological Sites" 063, Shinsensha, 2009 The mound shares its orientation with the Ushirofutago Kofun and was constructed around the same time, suggesting a close association with the individual buried in the Ushirofutago Kofun. During restoration efforts at the Ōmuro Burial Mounds, the Shōfutago Kofun underwent extensive excavation due to its poor preservation. Findings from these excavations allowed a faithful restoration of the mound, complete with re-erected haniwa to replicate its original appearance. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber is a semi-underground corridor-style stone chamber without sidewalls. Although the chamber was damaged by [[looting]] during the [[Meiji era]], it has been restored to its original dimensions: total length of 6 meters, back wall width of 1.8 meters, and height of 1.8 meters. Artifacts recovered include 14 glass beads, three gilt-bronze earrings, three [[straight swords]], one [[dagger]], ten [[iron arrowheads]], nine bow fittings, and one piece of [[Sue ware]]. Additional finds in front of the chamber included eight Haji ware bowls and one Sue ware jar, characteristic of the late 6th century. Burnt soil detected near the chamber suggests that funerary rituals were performed at the site. == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Ushirofutago and Small Burial Mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|529|Ushirofutago and Small Burial Mounds}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[Ōmuro Burial Mounds (Maebashi)]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shofutago Kofun}} * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Burial Mounds] - Maebashi City website [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:小二子古墳]]

A shield-shaped [[moat]] surrounds the mound, reaching 44 meters in total length when including the moat. Cylindrical [[haniwa]] were arranged on terraces (80–90 in total), while figurative haniwa depicting people were placed on the front section, and artifact-themed haniwa were positioned on the rear circular section.Maehara Toyo, "Cylindrical Haniwa at Shōfutago Kofun," in ''The Prestige of Eastern Japan’s Great Clans: The Ōmuro Burial Mounds [Gunma],'' Series "Learning from Archaeological Sites" 063, Shinsensha, 2009 The mound shares its orientation with the Ushirofutago Kofun and was constructed around the same time, suggesting a close association with the individual buried in the Ushirofutago Kofun. During restoration efforts at the Ōmuro Burial Mounds, the Shōfutago Kofun underwent extensive excavation due to its poor preservation. Findings from these excavations allowed a faithful restoration of the mound, complete with re-erected haniwa to replicate its original appearance. == Burial Facility == The burial chamber is a semi-underground corridor-style stone chamber without sidewalls. Although the chamber was damaged by [[looting]] during the [[Meiji era]], it has been restored to its original dimensions: total length of 6 meters, back wall width of 1.8 meters, and height of 1.8 meters. Artifacts recovered include 14 glass beads, three gilt-bronze earrings, three [[straight swords]], one [[dagger]], ten [[iron arrowheads]], nine bow fittings, and one piece of [[Sue ware]]. Additional finds in front of the chamber included eight Haji ware bowls and one Sue ware jar, characteristic of the late 6th century. Burnt soil detected near the chamber suggests that funerary rituals were performed at the site. == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Ushirofutago and Small Burial Mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.{{National Cultural Properties Database|401|529|Ushirofutago and Small Burial Mounds}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[Ōmuro Burial Mounds (Maebashi)]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shofutago Kofun}} * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Burial Mounds] - Maebashi City website [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shofutago Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:小二子古墳]]

{{Infobox Kofun | image = [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus
(Right: Rear Circular Section & Stone Chamber Entrance, Left: Front Rectangular Section) | name = Shōfutago Kofun (小二子古墳) | group = {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} | location = {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | size = Total mound length: 38m
Rear circular part: diameter 30m, height 5m
Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial_chamber = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue ware, Haji ware, weapons, accessories | designation = Included in the National Historic Site designation of {{ill|Atofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} and has been designated a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Atofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun designation.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! Name ! Shape ! Size ! Burial Facility ! Period |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Atofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human forms on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治時代}}, the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber dimensions are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m Artifacts unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue ware vessel Haji ware bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue ware jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[ja:小二子古墳]]

Period |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human forms on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治時代}}, the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber dimensions are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m Artifacts unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue ware vessel Haji ware bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue ware jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[ja:小二子古墳]]

Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:::Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:::History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:::Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:::National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:::dd]][[ja:小二子古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | image = [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus
(Right: Rear Circular Section & Stone Chamber Entrance, Left: Front Rectangular Section) | name = Shōfutago Kofun (小二子古墳) | group = {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} | location = {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | size = Total mound length: 38m
Rear circular part: diameter 30m, height 5m
Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial_chamber = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue ware, Haji ware, weapons, accessories | designation = Included in the National Historic Site designation of {{ill|Atofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} and has been designated a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun designation.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! Shape ! Size ! Burial Facility ! Period |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human forms on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治時代}}, the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber dimensions are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m Artifacts unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue ware vessel Haji ware bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue ware jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[ja:小二子古墳]]

Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja:小二子古墳]]

Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial_chamber = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue ware, Haji ware, weapons, accessories | designation = Included in the National Historic Site designation of {{ill|Atofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} and has been designated a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun designation.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! Shape ! Size ! Burial Facility ! Period |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human forms on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治時代}}, the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber dimensions are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m Artifacts unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue ware vessel Haji ware bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue ware jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kk]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja:小二子古墳]]

It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} and has been designated a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun designation.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==

Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human forms on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治時代}}, the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber dimensions are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m Artifacts unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue ware vessel Haji ware bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue ware jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:小二子古墳]]

Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:小二子古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | image = [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]

(Right: Rear Circular Section & Stone Chamber Entrance, Left: Front Rectangular Section) | name = Shōfutago Kofun (小二子古墳) | group = {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} | location = {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | size = Total mound length: 38m
Rear circular part: diameter 30m, height 5m
Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial_chamber = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue ware, Haji ware, weapons, accessories | designation = Included in the National Historic Site designation of {{ill|Atofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} and has been designated a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun designation.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! Shape ! Size ! Burial Facility ! Period |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human forms on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治時代}}, the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber dimensions are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m Artifacts unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue ware vessel Haji ware bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue ware jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[ja:小二子古墳]]

Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:横穴式石室]]

Tumulus
(Right: Rear Circular Section & Stone Chamber Entrance, Left: Front Rectangular Section) | name = Shōfutago Kofun (小二子古墳) | group = {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} | location = {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | size = Total mound length: 38m
Rear circular part: diameter 30m, height 5m
Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial_chamber = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue ware, Haji ware, weapons, accessories | designation = Included in the National Historic Site designation of {{ill|Atofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} and has been designated a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun designation.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! Shape ! Size ! Burial Facility ! Period |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human forms on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治時代}}, the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber dimensions are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m Artifacts unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue ware vessel Haji ware bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue ware jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human forms on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治時代}}, the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber dimensions are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m Artifacts unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue ware vessel Haji ware bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue ware jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

Period |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human forms on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治時代}}, the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber dimensions are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m Artifacts unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue ware vessel Haji ware bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue ware jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:New]]

Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial_chamber = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue ware, Haji ware, weapons, accessories | designation = Included in the National Historic Site designation of {{ill|Atofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} and has been designated a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun designation.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! Shape ! Size ! Burial Facility ! Period |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human forms on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治時代}}, the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber dimensions are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m Artifacts unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue ware vessel Haji ware bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue ware jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]]

Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]]
