It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} and has been designated a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun designation.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! Shape ! Size ! Burial Facility ! Period |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human forms on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治時代}}, the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber dimensions are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m Artifacts unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue ware vessel Haji ware bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue ware jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

Period |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human forms on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治時代}}, the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber dimensions are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m Artifacts unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}}== See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | image = [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun
(Right: Rear Circular Section & Stone Chamber Entrance, Left: Front Rectangular Section) | name = Shōfutago Kofun (小二子古墳) | group = {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} | location = {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | size = Total mound length: 38m
Rear circular part: diameter 30m, height 5m
Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial_chamber = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | designation = Included in the National Historic Site designation of {{ill|Atofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} and has been designated a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun designation.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! Shape ! Size ! Burial Facility ! Period |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human forms on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治時代}}, the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber dimensions are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m Artifacts unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}}== See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{Kofun navbar}}

(Right: Rear Circular Section & Stone Chamber Entrance, Left: Front Rectangular Section) | name = Shōfutago Kofun (小二子古墳) | group = {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} | location = {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | size = Total mound length: 38m
Rear circular part: diameter 30m, height 5m
Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial_chamber = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | designation = Included in the National Historic Site designation of {{ill|Atofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} and has been designated a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun designation.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! Shape ! Size ! Burial Facility ! Period |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human forms on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治時代}}, the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber dimensions are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m Artifacts unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! Shape ! area ! Burial Facility ! Period |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human forms on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治時代}}, the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber dimensions are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m Artifacts unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human forms on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治時代}}, the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber dimensions are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m Artifacts unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == other_designation == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | image = [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun
(Right: Rear Circular Section & Stone Chamber Entrance, Left: Front Rectangular Section) | name = Shōfutago Kofun (小二子古墳) | part_of = {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} | location = {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | size = Total mound length: 38m
Rear circular part: diameter 30m, height 5m
Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial_chamber = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | designation = Included in the National Historic Site designation of {{ill|Atofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} and has been designated a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun designation.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated other_designation Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! Period |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human forms on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治時代}}, the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber dimensions are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m Artifacts unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == other_designation == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | image = [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun
(Right: Rear Circular Section & Stone Chamber Entrance, Left: Front Rectangular Section) | name = Shōfutago Kofun (小二子古墳) | part_of = {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} | location = {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | size = Total mound length: 38m
Rear circular part: diameter 30m, height 5m
Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial_chamber = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | designation = Included in the National Historic Site designation of {{ill|Atofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} and has been designated a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun designation.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated other_designation Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! Period |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human forms on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治時代}}, the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber dimensions are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m Artifacts unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == other_designation == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump]]

Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == other_designation == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Kofun
(Right: Rear Circular Section & Stone Chamber Entrance, Left: Front Rectangular Section) | name = Shōfutago Kofun (小二子古墳) | part_of = {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} | location = {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | size = Total mound length: 38m
Rear circular part: diameter 30m, height 5m
Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial_chamber = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | designation = Included in the National other_designation designation of {{ill|Atofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} and has been designated a [[National other_designation of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun designation.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated other_designation Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! Period |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human forms on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治時代}}, the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber dimensions are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m Artifacts unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National other_designations: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National other_designations: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Kofun
(Right: Rear Circular Section & Stone Chamber Entrance, Left: Front Rectangular Section) | name = Shōfutago Kofun (小二子古墳) | part_of = {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} | location = {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | size = Total mound length: 38m
Rear circular part: diameter 30m, height 5m
Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial_chamber = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | artifacts = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | designation = Included in the National other_designation designation of {{ill|Atofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} and has been designated a [[National other_designation of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun designation.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated other_designation Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! Period |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human forms on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治時代}}, the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber dimensions are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National other_designations: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National other_designations: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | image = [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun
(Right: Rear Circular Section & Stone Chamber Entrance, Left: Front Rectangular Section) | name = Shōfutago Kofun (小二子古墳) | part_of = {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} | location = {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | size = Total mound length: 38m
Rear circular part: diameter 30m, height 5m
Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial_chamber = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | other_designation = Included in the National other_other_designation other_designation of {{ill|Atofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} and has been other_designation1 a [[National other_other_designation of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun other_designation.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated other_other_designation Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! Period |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治時代}}, the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == other_other_designation == === National other_other_designation === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National other_other_designations: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National other_other_designations of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == other_other_designation == === National other_other_designation === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National other_other_designations: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Kofun
(Right: Rear Circular Section & Stone Chamber Entrance, Left: Front Rectangular Section) | name = Shōfutago Kofun (小二子古墳) | part_of = {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} | location = {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | size = Total mound length: 38m
Rear circular part: diameter 30m, height 5m
Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial_chamber = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | other_designation = Included in the National other_other_designation other_designation of {{ill|Atofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} and has been other_designation1 a [[National other_other_designation of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and Shōfutago Kofun other_designation.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated other_other_designation Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! Period |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治時代}}, the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == other_other_designation == === National other_other_designation === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National other_other_designations: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National other_other_designations of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]]

Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National other_designations: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National other_designations of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]][[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods.
