Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | image = [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun
(Right: Rear Circular Section & Stone Chamber Entrance, Left: Front Rectangular Section) | name = Shōfutago Kofun (小二子古墳) | part_of = {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=draft|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Total mound length: 38m
Rear circular part: diameter 30m, height 5m
Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} | burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | Historic Site = Included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of {{ill|Atofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=draft|12=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and Shōfutago Kofun Historic Site.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Historic Site Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=draft|12=draft}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! built |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]]

Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | image = [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun
(Right: Rear Circular Section & Stone Chamber Entrance, Left: Front Rectangular Section) | name = Shōfutago Kofun (小二子古墳) | part_of = {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=draft|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Total mound length: 38m
Rear circular part: diameter 30m, height 5m
Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} | burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | Historic Site = Included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of {{ill|Atofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=draft|12=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and Shōfutago Kofun Historic Site.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Historic Site Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=draft|12=draft}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! built |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]]

Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | image = [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun
(Right: Rear Circular Section & Stone Chamber Entrance, Left: Front Rectangular Section) | name = Shōfutago Kofun (小二子古墳) | part_of = {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=draft|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Total mound length: 38m
Rear circular part: diameter 30m, height 5m
Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} | burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | Historic Site = Included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of {{ill|Atofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=draft|12=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and Shōfutago Kofun Historic Site.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Historic Site Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=draft|12=draft}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! built |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]]

{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=draft|12=draft}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! built |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]]

Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]]

revision.comment: 23 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:小二子古墳 up to rev 92654083
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |画像=[[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘
（右に後円部・石室開口部、左に前方部） |名称=小二子古墳 |所属=[[大室古墳群 (前橋市)|大室古墳群]] |所在地=[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]西大室町2545 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長38m前橋市教育委員会編「前橋市　大室古墳群 -前二子古墳・中二子古墳・後二子古墳・小二子古墳-」、2013年、後円部径30m、高さ5m、前方部幅18m |築造時期=[[6世紀]]後半 |埋葬施設=[[横穴式石室]] |出土品=円筒埴輪、形象埴輪、須恵器、土師器、武器、装身具 |史跡=国の[[史跡]]「後二子古墳ならびに小古墳」に包含 }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|墳丘}}]] '''小二子古墳'''（しょうふたごこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]西大室町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[大室古墳群 (前橋市)|大室古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。[[後二子古墳]]と合わせて国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（指定名称は「後二子古墳ならびに小古墳」）[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index 国指定文化財等データベース　文化庁]。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+[[大室古墳群 (前橋市)|大室古墳群]]の前方後円墳4基 !古墳名 !形状 !規模 !埋葬施設 !築造時期 |- |[[前二子古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長94m||両袖式[[横穴式石室]]||[[6世紀]]初頭 |- |[[中二子古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長111m||横穴式石室?||6世紀前半 |- |[[後二子古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長85m||両袖式横穴式石室||6世紀後半 |- |'''小二子古墳'''||前方後円墳||墳丘長38m||無袖式横穴式石室||6世紀後半 |}
墳丘長38メートルの前方後円墳で、主軸を北東131°に向けている。墳丘は2段構築となっている。墳丘の一部は地山を削り出して造成され、[[葺石]]は施されていない。墳丘の周囲は盾型の[[堀]]が巡らされ、堀を入れた長さは44メートルに達する。テラス面には80～90本の[[円筒埴輪]]列、前方部頂には人物を中心とした形象埴輪列、後円部頂には器財を中心にした形象埴輪が配列されていた前原豊 「埴輪が林立する小二子古墳」『東国大豪族の威勢・大室古墳群〔群馬〕』 （シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」063）、新泉社、2009年。 後二子古墳とほぼ同様の東西軸を持ち、つくられた時期も同じ頃で、後二子古墳と関わりの深い人物の墓と考えられる。 大室古墳群整備の際、小二子古墳は墳丘の残りがよくなかったため、築造時の状況を復元し展示する資料を得る目的で全面的な[[発掘調査]]が行われた。現在は調査結果を基に築造時のように墳丘に埴輪が樹立された状態に復元されている。 == 埋葬施設 == 半地下式の無袖型横穴式石室である。石室は[[明治時代]]に[[盗掘]]を受け、天井部分が破壊されていたが、全長6メートル、奥壁幅1.8メートル、高さ1.8メートルと復元されている。ガラス製小玉14、金銅製耳環3、[[直刀]]3、[[刀子]]1、[[鉄鏃]]10、[[弓]]金具9、[[須恵器]]1が出土した。石室前面からは[[6世紀]]後半の特徴を示す[[土師器]]坏8と須恵器提瓶1が出土している。石室両側からは焼土が検出され、墓前祭祀が行われたことをうかがわせる。 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 後二子古墳ならびに小古墳 - 1927年（昭和2年）4月8日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|529|後二子古墳ならびに小古墳}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[大室古墳群 (前橋市)]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shofutago Kofun}} * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html 大室古墳群] - 前橋市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:しようふたここふん}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:前橋市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:群馬県にある国指定の史跡]] {{japanese-history-stub|}}

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | image = [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun
(Right: Rear Circular Section & Stone Chamber Entrance, Left: Front Rectangular Section) | name = Shōfutago Kofun (小二子古墳) | part_of = {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | location = {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Total mound length: 38m
Rear circular part: diameter 30m, height 5m
Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | Historic Site = Included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of {{ill|Atofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Shōfutago Kofun Historic Site.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Historic Site Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! built |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]] {{translated page|ja|小二子古墳|version=92654083|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Rear circular part: diameter 30m, height 5m
Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | Historic Site = Included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of {{ill|Atofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Shōfutago Kofun Historic Site.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Historic Site Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! built |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小二子古墳|version=92654083|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]]

Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | Historic Site = Included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of {{ill|Atofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Shōfutago Kofun Historic Site.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Historic Site Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! built |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小二子古墳|version=92654083|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]]

It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushirofutago Kofun|qq=}} and Shōfutago Kofun Historic Site.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Historic Site Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! built |- |{{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maefutago Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakafutago Kofun|qq=}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushirofutago Kofun|qq=}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukiishi|qq=}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小二子古墳|version=92654083|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]]

Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小二子古墳|version=92654083|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]]

Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | Historic Site = Included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of {{ill|Atofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atofutago Kofun|qq=}} and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi|qq=}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushirofutago Kofun|qq=}} and Shōfutago Kofun Historic Site.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Historic Site Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! built |- |{{ill|Mae Futago Kofun|lt=Mae Futago Kofun|lt=Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maefutago Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakafutago Kofun|qq=}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushirofutago Kofun|qq=}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukiishi|qq=}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小二子古墳|version=92654083|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]]

Rear circular part: diameter 30m, height 5m
Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | Historic Site = Included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of {{ill|Atofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atofutago Kofun|qq=}} and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi|qq=}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushirofutago Kofun|qq=}} and Shōfutago Kofun Historic Site.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Historic Site Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! built |- |{{ill|Mae Futago Kofun|lt=Mae Futago Kofun|lt=Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maefutago Kofun|qq=}}||{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |{{ill|Naka Futago Kofun|lt=Naka Futago Kofun|lt=Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakafutago Kofun|qq=}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |{{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushirofutago Kofun|qq=}}||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukiishi|qq=}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小二子古墳|version=92654083|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]]
