Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No {{ill|Fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukiishi|qq=}} (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小二子古墳|version=92654083|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]]

Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | Historic Site = Included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Atofutago Kofun]] and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and Shōfutago Kofun Historic Site.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Historic Site Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! built |- |[[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]]||[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |[[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]]||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |[[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小二子古墳|version=92654083|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]]

Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小二子古墳|version=92654083|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:前橋市の歴史]] [[Category:群馬県にある国指定の史跡]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | image = [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun
(Right: Rear Circular Section & Stone Chamber Entrance, Left: Front Rectangular Section) | name = Shōfutago Kofun (小二子古墳) | part_of = {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total mound length: 38m
Rear circular part: diameter 30m, height 5m
Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | Historic Site = Included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Atofutago Kofun]] and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and Shōfutago Kofun Historic Site.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Historic Site Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! built |- |[[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]]||[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |[[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]]||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |[[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小二子古墳|version=92654083|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:前橋市の歴史]] [[Category:群馬県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:群馬県の古墳]]

(Right: Rear Circular Section & Stone Chamber Entrance, Left: Front Rectangular Section) | name = Shōfutago Kofun (小二子古墳) | part_of = {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total mound length: 38m
Rear circular part: diameter 30m, height 5m
Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[6th century]] | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | Historic Site = Included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Atofutago Kofun]] and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and Shōfutago Kofun Historic Site.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Historic Site Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! built |- |[[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]]||[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}||Early [[6th century]] |- |[[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]]||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |[[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小二子古墳|version=92654083|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:History of Maebashi, Gunma]] [[Category:群馬県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]]

No [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小二子古墳|version=92654083|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:History of Maebashi, Gunma]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shofutago Kofun}}

Rear circular part: diameter 30m, height 5m
Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | Historic Site = Included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Atofutago Kofun]] and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and Shōfutago Kofun Historic Site.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Historic Site Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! built |- |[[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]]||[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}||Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] |- |[[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]]||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |[[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小二子古墳|version=92654083|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:History of Maebashi, Gunma]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shofutago Kofun}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | image = [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun
(Right: Rear Circular Section & Stone Chamber Entrance, Left: Front Rectangular Section) | name = Shōfutago Kofun (小二子古墳) | part_of = {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total mound length: 38m
Rear circular part: diameter 30m, height 5m
Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | Historic Site = Included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Atofutago Kofun]] and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and Shōfutago Kofun Historic Site.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Historic Site Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! built |- |[[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]]||[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}||Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] |- |[[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]]||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |[[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小二子古墳|version=92654083|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shofutago Kofun}}

Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | Historic Site = Included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Atofutago Kofun]] and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and Shōfutago Kofun Historic Site.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Historic Site Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! built |- |[[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]]||[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}||Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] |- |[[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]]||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |[[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小二子古墳|version=92654083|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shofutago Kofun}}

Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小二子古墳|version=92654083|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shofutago Kofun}}

(Right: Rear Circular Section & Stone Chamber Entrance, Left: Front Rectangular Section) | name = Shōfutago Kofun (小二子古墳) | part_of = {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|22.52|N|139|11|37.53|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total mound length: 38m
Rear circular part: diameter 30m, height 5m
Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | Historic Site = Included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Atofutago Kofun]] and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and Shōfutago Kofun Historic Site.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Historic Site Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! built |- |[[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]]||[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}||Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] |- |[[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]]||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |[[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小二子古墳|version=92654083|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shofutago Kofun}}

Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | Historic Site = Included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Atofutago Kofun]] and Shōfutago Kofun }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and Shōfutago Kofun Historic Site.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Historic Site Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! built |- |[[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]]||[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}||Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] |- |[[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]]||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |[[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小二子古墳|version=92654083|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shofutago Kofun}}

Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小二子古墳|version=92654083|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shofutago Kofun}}

Rear circular part: diameter 30m, height 5m
Front rectangular part: width 18m | period = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = Cylindrical haniwa, figurative haniwa, Sue pottery, Haji pottery, weapons, accessories | Historic Site = Included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Atofutago Kofun]] and Shōfutago Kofun | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:Shofutago Kofun, funkyu-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mound View}}]] '''Shōfutago Kofun''' (小二子古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the tumuli that make up the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and Shōfutago Kofun Historic Site.[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Nationally Designated Historic Site Database - Agency for Cultural Affairs] == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+{{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}}: Four Zenpokoenfun ! name ! type ! area ! burial ! built |- |[[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]]||[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]||Mound length 94m||Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}||Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] |- |[[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]]||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 111m||Suspected Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||First half of the 6th century |- |[[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 85m||Double-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |- |'''Shōfutago Kofun'''||Zenpokoenfun||Mound length 38m||Single-sleeved Yokoana-style Stone Chamber||Late 6th century |}
Shōfutago Kofun has a total mound length of 38 meters, oriented along a northeast axis of 131°. The mound consists of two tiers, partially built by excavating the natural terrain. No [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]] (stone paving) was applied. The mound is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, with the overall length including the moat reaching 44 meters. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged in rows (80–90 pieces) on the terrace, with figurative haniwa of human types on the front section and vessel-shaped haniwa on the rear section.{{cite book|author=Toyohiko Maehara|title=Haniwa Forests: Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Shinsensha|year=2009}} The alignment and construction period of Shōfutago Kofun correspond to those of Atofutago Kofun, suggesting a close relationship between the two burial sites. Shōfutago Kofun underwent full excavation as part of the preservation and study of the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Findings were used to restore the mound, complete with haniwa, to resemble its original state. == burial == The burial facility is a semi-subterranean single-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Although the ceiling was destroyed during tomb robbery in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], the chamber has been reconstructed. The chamber area are: * Total length: 6m * Rear wall width: 1.8m * Height: 1.8m burial goods unearthed include: * 14 glass beads * 3 gilt bronze earrings * 3 iron swords * 1 dagger * 10 iron arrowheads * 9 bow fittings * 1 Sue pottery vessel Haji pottery bowls (8 pieces) and a Sue pottery jar (1 piece), characteristic of the late 6th century, were found in front of the chamber. Burnt earth from both sides of the chamber suggests funerary rituals were conducted at the site. == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun – Designated on April 8, 1927.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=National Historic Sites: Atofutago Kofun and Shōfutago Kofun|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群 (前橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5109.html Ōmuro Kofun Cluster - Official Website of Maebashi City] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小二子古墳|version=92654083|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]][[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sho Futago Kofun]] [[ja:小二子古墳]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shofutago Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17215924}}
